evidence: Job 1910599 on nvl72d022-T13 reached the runtime-bootstrap announcement, then task 0 exited 1 before any curl or uv output; elapsed time was 00:02:23.
hypothesis: The container runtime did not receive a valid writable temporary directory.
reproduction: Submit the functional wrapper on aws_dfw with the login image's stale TMPDIR environment.
root_cause: Diagnostic job 1911208 showed that bash -lc restored a stale login-image TMPDIR after the /runtime mount was established.
symptom: Functional job failed silently at runtime_bootstrap before curl.
tested_change: Instrument the host/Pyxis/login-shell boundary and verify the /runtime mount independently of TMPDIR.
verification_evidence: Job 1911208 isolated the post-login environment overwrite; the production fix still requires a new remote verification job.

evidence: Job 1911208 on nvl72d042-T04 found /runtime/tmp mounted from local NVMe, root:root mode 755 and writable as uid 0, while bash -lc observed TMPDIR=/lustre/fsw/portfolios/nemotron/projects/nemotron_sw_post/users/sna/tmp; equality with /runtime/tmp failed and touching the stale path returned ENOENT.
hypothesis: Pyxis propagation alone could override the image's stale temporary-directory value.
reproduction: Start the aws_dfw Pyxis image with host TMPDIR=/runtime/tmp and inspect TMPDIR inside bash -lc.
root_cause: Login-image initialization overwrote TMPDIR after Pyxis environment setup; the mount and permissions were not the failure.
symptom: A valid /runtime/tmp mount existed, but the login shell selected a nonexistent Lustre TMPDIR.
tested_change: Keep host TMPDIR at the host runtime path, stop propagating TMPDIR through Pyxis, and export /runtime/tmp as the first command inside every container shell.
verification_evidence: Job 1911208 proved the boundary diagnosis; host regression tests simulate the stale post-login value, while remote verification remains pending.
